items_en: The Bnited Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization, having regard to the obligation of the United Nations to promote the objectives set forth in Article 55 of the Chart~ and the function and power of the Council, under Article 62 of the Charter, to make or initiate studies and reports with respect to inter. rational economic, social, cultural, educational, health and related outcomes and' to make recommend› recommendations concerning these matters to the specialized agencies concerned, and having re-' gard, also, to the responsibility of the United NatioQ.S, under Articles 58 and 63 of the Charter, to make recommendations for the co-ordination . of the policies and activities of its specialized agencies, agreements t9 arranged for the submission, as soon as possible, to the appropriate organization of the United Nations.

The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization, having regard to the obligation of the United Nations to promote the achievement of the objectives laid down in Article 55 of the Charter, and the functions of the Council referred to in Article 62 of the Charter, to make or provoke studies and reports on international economic, social, cultural, educational and other matters relating to public health and other matters, and to make recommendations on all these matters to the specialized institutions; and having regard also to the mission of the United Nations, under Articles 58 and 63 of the Charter, to make recommendations with a view to coordinating the programmes and activities of the specialized institutions, it is appropriate to take all measures with a view to submitting, in the shortest possible time, to its competent organ, any recommendations which the United Nations may make to it.

The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization agreements to enter into · c-0nsultaμon. with the United Nations upon re› quest with. respect to such recommendations, and in due time to report to the l)nied Nations on the action taken by the Organization ''or by its members to give effect to such, recom› recommendations, or -on the other results of their consideration.

The United Natiop.s Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization affirms its intention of co-operating in what further measures may be necessary to make co-ordination of the activities of specialized agencies and those of the United Nations fully effective. In particular, it agrees to participate in, and to co-operate with, any body or bodies which the Council may estab› lish for the purpose of facilitating such eo-ordi› nation and tofurnish such information as· may be required. for the .;:arrying out of this purpose.

The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization shall exchange views with the United Nations, at its request, on these recommendations and shall, in due course, report to the United Nations on the measures taken by the Organization or its Members with a view to sleeping with those recommendations, OR SUR TOUB other results which would have followed the taking into account of these recommendations.

The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization affirms its intention to cooperate with all necessary measures to ensure the effective coordination of the activities of the specialized institutions and of the United Nations.

items_en: 1. The United Nations and the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization recognize that, in order to unify administrative and technical methods and to make the best possible use of staff and resources, it is desirable to avoid the creation of services within the United Nations and specialized agencies that compete or duplicate. · -

3. Pending  the  conclusion  of  such agreement, the following arrangements shall govern budget› ary  and  financial  relationships  between  the United Nations aIId the United Nations Educa› tional,  Scientific  and Cultural  Organization:
(a) Ih the preparation of the budget of the United  Nations  Educational,  Scientific  and Cultural Organization, the Organization shall . consult  with the United Nations.

( b) The United Nations Educational, Sci› entific  and  Cultural  Organization  agrees ·.to transmit  its  proposed  budget  to  the  United Nations  annually  at the  same  time  as  such budget  is  transmitted  to  its  members. The General  Assembly  shall  examine  the  budget or proposed  budget of the  Organization and may make recommendations to it concerning any item or items' contained therein.

'( c) Representatives of the United Nations, Educational,  Scientific  and  Cultural  Organi› zation shall be entitled to participate, without vote,  in  the I delibe.rations  of  the  General Assembly  or  any  committee  thereof  at  all . times when the budget of the Organization or general  administrative  or  financ;ial  questions affecting  the  Organization  are  under  con› sideration.

(d) The  United  Nations  may  undertake the  collection  of  contributions  from  those members of the United Nations Educational, Scientific  and  Cultural  Qrganization  which are  also  Members  of the  United  Nations  in accordance  with  such  arrangements  as  may be defined  by a  later agreement between  the United Natiops and the Organization.

( e) The United Nations shall, upon its own initiative  or  upon  the  request  of the United Nations  Educational,  Scientific  and  Cultural Organization, arrange for studies to be under› taken  ~oncerning  other  financial  and  fiscal questions of interest to the Organization  and to  other specialized  agencies  with  a  view  to the  provision  of  common  services  and  the securing  of  uniformity in such matters.

(/)  The  .United Nations Educational, Scientific  and  Cultural  Organization  agrees to  conform,  as  far . as  may be practicable,  to standard  practices  and  forms  recommended . by the  United  Nations.
items_en: 1. The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization recognizes the desirability of close budgetary and financial relations with the United Nations in order to ensure that the administrative work of the United Nations and the specialized agencies is carried out in a manner that is most efficient and cost-effective and that the maximum amount of coordination and unity in this work is ensured.

2. The United Nations and the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization agree to cooperate as far as possible in order to achieve these objectives and in particular to exchange views in order to conclude the arrangements which are appropriate for the inclusion of the Organization's budget in a general budget of the United Nations. These arrangements will be defined in a complementary agreement between the two organizations.

3. Pending the conclusion of such agreement, the following arrangements will governance budget› ary and financial relationships between the United Nations aIId the United Nations Educational› regional, Scientific and Cultural Organization:

3. Pending the conclusion of this Agreement, the following provisions shall govern the budgetary and financial relations between the United Nations and the United Nations for the purposes of the establishment, science and culture:
· (a) During the preparation of the budget for the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization, the Organization shall exchange views with the United Nations.

(b) The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization shall agree to hold its budget proposal to the United Nations on an annual basis at the same time as it communicates it to its members. The General Assembly shall examine the budget OR the draft budget of the Organization and may make recommendations to the Organization concerning one or more items of that budget.

(c) Representatives of the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization shall have the right to participate, without the right to vote, in the deliberations of the General Assembly OR of one of its committees whenever the budget of the Organization or of general administrative or financial matters of interest to the Organization is examined.

(d) The United Nations will be able to undertake the collection of the contributions of the members of the United Nations for education, science and culture, who are also Members of the United Nations, in accordance with the arrangements to be established, as appropriate, in a later agreement between the United Nations and the United Nations.

(e) The United Nations shall, on its own initiative, or at the request of the United Nations for education, science and culture, take steps to undertake studies on financial and fiscal matters of interest to the Organization and other specialized institutions, with a view to establishing common services and ensuring uniformity in these fields;
(f) The United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization agrees to comply, as far as possible, with United Nations practices and rules recommended by the United Nations.

items_en: 1. The United Nations and the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations agree to cooperate as fully as possible in order to avoid unnecessary duplication and to use their technical personnel with the utmost efficiency in their respective activities concerning the collection, analysis, publication and dissemination of statistical information.

2. The United Nations Food and Agriculture Organization recognizes that the United Nations is the central agency responsible for collecting, analysing, publishing, standardizing and advancing statistics for the general purposes of international organizations.

3. The United Nations Organization for Food and Agriculture is recognized by the United Nations as being the organisrne appro› requests to collect, analyse, publish, standardise and advance statistics in its own field, without prejudice to the right of the United Nations to strengthen such statistics as long as they are essential for the pursuit of their own purposes and the development of statistics throughout the world.

4. The Unitecl Nations will in· consultation with the specialized agencies develop adminis› tative instruments . and procedures through which effective statistical co~operation may be secured between the United Nations and the agencies bought into relationship with it.

4. The United Nations shall establish, in consultation with the specialized institutions, the administrative instruments and procedures by which effective cooperation between the United Nations and its related institutions can be ensured.

5. It is recognized as desirable that the collection of statistical information should not be duplicated by the United Nations or any of the specialized ag~nces when it is practical for any of them to use information or materials which another may have available. ·

5. It is recognized that it is desirable that statistical information be not collected simultaneously by the United Nations and by any specialized institution whenever it is possible to use the information and information that another institution can provide.

6. In order to build up a central collection of statistical information for general use, it is agreed that \_data suppHed to the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations for incorporation in its basic statistical series or special report should, so far as practicable;, be made available to the U:μated Nations.

6. In order to establish a centre for the collection of statistical information intended for general use, it is recognised that the data provided to the United Nations for the purposes of food and agriculture for inclusion in its basic statistical series and its special reports will, to the extent possible, be made available to the United Nations.

3. Pending the conclusion of this Agreement, the following provisions shall govern the budgetary and financial relations between the United Nations and the Organization:
(a) The Secretary General and the Director General shall exchange views on the preparation of the budget for the United Nations Food and Agriculture Organization.

(b) The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations agrees to submit annually to the United Nations its budget proposal at the same time as it communicates it to its members. The General Assembly shall examine the budget or draft budget of the Organization and may make such recommendations as it deems necessary.

(c) Representatives of the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations shall have the right to participate, without the right to vote, in the deliberations of the Assembly or any committee thereof, at any time or shall be examined by the Organization OR by any administrative or financial matters affecting the Organization.

(d) The United Nations shall be able to undertake the recovery of the contributions of the members of the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations, which shall also be members of the United Nations.

(e) The United Nations shall, on its own initiative or on request of the United Nations Food and Agriculture Organization and other specialized institutions, arrange for studies on financial and financial matters with a view to establishing and ensuring uniformity in these fields.
(f) The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations agrees to comply, to the extent possible, with the uniform practices and rules recommended by the United Nations.

items_fr: 1. items_en: 1. In the event that the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations would have to meet significant additional expenses made necessary by a request for reports, studies or special assistance submitted by the United Nations under Articles V, VI, VII or any other provision of this Agreement, the Organization and the United Nations shall exchange views in order to determine the most equitable way to meet these expenses.

items_fr: 2. Consultation  between  the  United  Nations and the Food  and Agriculture  Organization  of the  United  Nations  shall  similarly  take  place with  a  view  to  making  such  arrangements  as may be found equitable for  covering the cost of central administrative, technical or fiscal  services or facilities  or other special  assistance  provided by the United Nations.
items_en: 2. Consultation between the United Nations and the Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations will similarly take place with a view to making such arrangements as may be found equitable for covering the cost of central administrative, technical or fiscal services or other special assistance provided by the United Nations.

2. The provisions relating to the links provided for. to the preceding articles of this Agreement shall apply, to the extent possible, both to the relations between the regional and local offices and the two organizations, and to the relations between their central administrations.

items_fr: 1. In accordance with its terms of  reference as defined  in  the resolution  of  the  Council  on  16 February 1946, the Committee concluded nego› tiations with the International Labour Organiza› tion,  the  United Nations Educational,  Scientific and  Cultural  Organization,  and  the  Food  and Agriculture  Organization, with a  view  to· bring› ing these organizatio:qs into relationship with the United Nations.

2. The  Committee  met  with  the  negotiating · delegation  of  the International Labour  Organi› za.tion  on  28  and  29  May.  A  working  paper ( E/NSA/2)  prepared at the secretarial level of both organizations  was  taken  as  a  basis  for  dis› cussion.  · As  the  result  of · the  negotiations,  the draft agreement between the United Nations and the International Labour Organization,  as  con. tained  in  document  E/48/Rev. 1,  was  accepted by  both negotiating committees.

3. The  Committee  met  with  the  negotiating delegation  of  the  United  Nations  Educational, Scientific  and Cultural Organization on 3 June, and  after  a  full  discussion,  a  draft  agreement between  the  United  Nations  and  the  United Nations  Educational,  Scientific  and  Cultural Orgariization,  as  contained in document  E/53, was drawn up and accepted by both negotiating committees.

4. The negotiations  with the negotiating  dele› gation of the Food and Agriculture Organization took  place  on 6  and  7  June.  The draft  agree› ment between the United Nations and the Food and  Agriculture  Organization,  as  contained  in document E/5 7,  was accepted by both negotiat› ing committees.

3. items_fr: Conformement  au  mandat  que  le  Conseil lui  a  confie  par  sa  resolution  en  date  du  16 fevrier  1946,  le  Comite a  acheve avec !'Organi› sation  intemationale  du  Travail, !'Organisation des  Nations  Unies ·pour !'education, la science et la culture,  et !'Organisation de l'alimentation et  de  !'agriculture, les  negociations· qui  avaient pour but de  mettre ces  i,nstitutions  en  relations avec  l'Organisation des  Nations  Unies.

2. Le Comite s'est reuni avec la delegation  de l'Organisation internationale  du  travail  les  28 et  29  mai. Un document de travail  (E/NSA/2), elabore  par  les  secretariats  des  deux  organisa› tions,  a  servi  de  base  aux discussions.  Les  deux comites de  negociations ont pu, a l'issue de cette reunion, adopter en commun un projet d'accord entre !'Organisation des  Nations  Unies  et  }'Or› ganisation  internationale  du  Travail,  qui  fait l'objet du  document  E/48/Rev.1.

Le Comite s'.est reuni avec  la delegation  de !'Organisation des  Nations  Unies  pour !'educa› tion, la science et la culture le 3 juin.  Apres une discussion  approfondie,  le  projet d'accord entre !'Organisation des  Nations  Unies  et, !'Organisa› tion des Nations  Unies  pour  !'education,  la science et la culture, reproduit dans le  document E/53, a ete redige et adopte par les  deux comites de  negociations.

4. Les  negociations  avec  la delegation  de !'Or› ganisation  de  l'alimentation et  de  !'agriculture ont eu lieu  les  6 et 7  juin.  Les  deux  comites de negociations  ont adopte le. projet d'accord entre !'Organisation des  Nations  Unies  et !'Organisa› tion de l'alimentation et de !'agriculture qui fait l'objet du  document  E/57.
items_en: In accordance with the mandate entrusted to it by the Council by its resolution of 16 February 1946, the Committee completed with the 'Organi' of the International Labour Organization, the United Nations Organization for Education, Science and Culture, and the Organization for Food and Agriculture, the negotiations which were intended to put these institutions into relations with the United Nations.

2. The Committee met with the delegation of the International Labour Organization on 28 and 29 May. A working paper (E/NSA/2), drawn up by the secretariats of the two organizations, served as the basis for the discussions. The two negotiating committees were able, at the end of the meeting, to jointly adopt a draft agreement between the United Nations and the International Labour Organization, which is contained in document E/48/Rev.1.

The Committee met with the delegation of the United Nations for education, science and culture on 3 June. After an in-depth discussion, the draft agreement between the United Nations and the United Nations for education, science and culture, reproduced in document E/53, was drafted and adopted by the two negotiating committees.

4. The negotiations with the delegation of the Organization for Food and Agriculture took place on 6 and 7 June. The two negotiating committees adopted the draft agreement between the United Nations and the Organization for Food and Agriculture, which is the subject of document E/57.

text_fr: Les  paragraphes · 2 · et  3  relatifs  au  mandat de  la  Commission  defini  par  la  resolution  du Conseil  en date  du · 16  f evrier  1946  ( document E/28)  sont rem places  par les  suivants:
text_en: Paragraphs 2 and 3 relating to the Commission's mandate as defined by the Council Resolution of 16 February 1946 (document E/28) are replaced by the following:

items_fr: (a) The Comm~sion shall advise the Eco› . nomic  and Social  Council.on economic  ques› tions in order to  promote higher ·standards of living.

( b) It shall examine such questions as may be submitted  to  it  by  the  Council,  and  shall on  its  own initiative r'eport to the Council on problems which, in its opinion, require urgent attention. · ·

( c) It shall  make recommendations to the Council with. reference to  economic questions involving  concerted study\_ and  (or)  action by more than one specialized agency or commis› sion  of  the  Council,  and  in  particular  shall draw , the  attention  of  the  Council  to  the probable·influence of the policies and activities of  other  commissions  of  the  Council,  the specialized  agencies  or  other  · international organizations  on  the  i~ues  mentioned  in paragraph ( d) below.
· ( d) In particular, it shall be  the function of the Commission to  advise the Council on:
( i) The prevention of wide fluctuations in economic activity and the promotion of full employment by the co-ordination of national full  employment  policies  and  by  international action; · ·

(ii) Problems  of  the  reconstruction  of devastated  areas  and  other  urgent  prob› lems  arising  from  the war,  with  a  view  to developing means .of giving real help, which is  so  neq:ssary,  to  various  Members  of  the United Nations whose  territories .have  been devastated  by  the  enemy  as  a  result  of occupation and war activities; ·
(iii)  The promotion of  economic  devel› opment and progress  with special regard to the problems  of less-developed  areas.

text_en: In carrying out the functions set forth above, the Commission will take account of the close · relationship between. the short-term problems and the long-term objectives of an expan~ing and integrated world economy.

items_fr: (a) A  sub-commission  on employment  to study  national  and,  in  consultation  with the sub-commissions  on balance of  payments and economic  development,  international methods · of  promoting  ·full  employment  and  relatep problems,  and to  analyse  information regard› . ing  employment  and  unemployment  in  the .  various countries;

· d) En  particulier,  la  Commission  a  pour attribution  de  fournir  des  avis  au Conseil  en ce  qui concerne':

i)  Les mesures necessaires pour ,eviter des fluctuations economiques excessives  et pour favoriser  le  plein  emploi . en  coordonnant les  programmes des diverses nations tendant a .ce  but  et  en  prenant  des  dispositions d' ordre  international;

ii)  Les  problemes  concernant  la recons› truction  des  pays  devastes,  ainsi  que  les autres  problemes  urgents  resultant  qe  la guerre,  en vue de fournir l'assistance effec› tive  qui  leur  est  si  necessaire  aux  divers Membres des  Nations  Unies dont les  terri› toires  ont ete  devastes  par l'ennemi du fait de  la guerre  et  de- l'occupation;

items_en: (a) A subcommission on employment to study national and, in consultation with the subcommissions on balance of payments and economic development, international methods · of promoting ·full employment and relationp problems, and to analyse information regard› . ing employment and unemployment in the . various countries;
(a) The Commission shall give opinions to the Economic and Social Council on economic matters with a view to promoting the raising of living standards.

(b) The Commission shall examine matters which are submitted to it by the Council and shall, on its own initiative, report to the Council on problems which, in the opinion of the Commission, require immediate attention.

(c) The Commission shall submit to the Council mandates relating to economic matters which require the study and/or concerted action of more than one specialised institution OR committee of the Council, and shall draw the Council's attention in particular to the effect that the objectives and activities of other committees of the Council, specialized institutions and other international organizations may have on the matters referred to in paragraph (d) below.
(d) In particular, the Commission has the task of providing advice to the Council on:

(i) The measures necessary to avoid excessive economic fluctuations and to promote full employment by coordinating the programmes of the various nations for that purpose and by making international arrangements;

(ii) Problems concerning the reconstitution of the countries of destruction, as well as other urgent problems resulting from war, with a view to providing the necessary effective assistance to the various Members of the United Nations whose territories have been destroyed by the enemy as a result of war and occupation;
(iii) The problems of development and economic progress, in particular those relating to forward-hand regions.

items_en: 2. The Sub-Commission shall have the mandate to advise on: ·

(a) The nature and fate of the problems of economic reconstruction which arise in countries which have had to carry out serious and urgent tasks in this field, either as a result of the occupation which they have suffered or of the material devastation which has been inflicted upon them;
(b) Progress in reconstruction and international cooperation measures to facilitate and accelerate the effective reconstruction of these countries.

3. The Sub-Commission shall be authorized to carry out investigations, with the exception of Germany and Japan, with a view to the preparation of a preliminary report which shall be drawn up by 2 September 1946 (no later than 2 September 1946, concerning the problems of economic reconstruction in the countries concerned, paying special attention to the requests of the Member States of the United Nations. In studying the problems of reconstruction in those countries, the Sub-Commission shall take account of their economic relations with Germany and Japan and shall obtain the necessary information through the intermediary of the Governments of the occupying Powers. Relations with the neutral countries shall also be taken into account and, whenever possible, the Commission shall ensure the cooperation of those countries.
4. The Secretary-General is authorized to divide the Sub-Commission into two working groups,

items_fr: 1.  · A  permanent  Transport  and  Communications  Commission shall be established to replace the Temporary Transport and Communications Commission. ,
1. II  sera cree  U:ne  Commission  des  transports et  des  communications  dC';Stinee a remplacer  la Commission  temporaire  des  transports  et  des communications.
(a) · The  Transport · and  Communications Commission shall consist of one representative from  each of  fifteen  Members  of  the  United Nations  selected  by  the  Council.

(b) Wit)l  a  view  to  securing  a  balanced representation  in  the  various  fields  covered by  the  Commission,  the . Secretary-General shall consult with the Governments so selected before  the  representatives  are  finally  nomi› nated by these Governments and confirmed by the  Council. . ·

( c) Except for the initial  period,  the term of  office  shall  be  for'  three  years. For  the initial  period,  one-third  of  the members  shall serve for two  years,  one-third  for three years and one-third for four years, the term of each member to be determined by lot.
( d) Retiring members shall be eligible  for re-election. ·

( e) In  the  event  that  a  member  of  the · Commission  is  unable · to  serve  for  the  full three-year term, the vacancy thus arising shall .be filled  by a  representative designated by the .. Member  Government,  subject  to·  the  pro› ., visions  of  paragraph  ( b) above.
items_en: 1. · A permanent Transport and Communications Commission shall be established to replace the Temporary Transport and Communications Commission. ,
1. It will be established the Committee on Transport and Communications;Stinee to replace the Temporary Committee on Transport and Communications.
(a) · The Transport · and Communications Commission shall consist of one representative from each of fifteen Members of the United Nations selected by the Council.

(b) Wit)l a view to secure a balanced representation in the various fields covered by the Commission, the . Secretary-General shall consult with the Governments so selected before the representatives are finally nominated› born by these Governments and confirmed by the Council. ·

(c) Except for the initial period, the term of office shall be for' three years. For the initial period, one-third of the members shall serve for two years, one-third for three years and one-third for four years, the term of each member to be determined by lot.
(d) Withdrawing members shall be eligible for re-election. ·

(e) In the event that a member of the · Commission is unable · to serve for the full three-year term, the vacuum thus arising shall .be daughter by a representative designed by the .. Member Government, subject to· the pro› ., visions of paragraph ( b) above.

items_en: (a) T,o assist the Economic ·and Social Council in its Tasks concerned with transport and communications problems;
(b) To advise the Council on the co-ordinate› nation of the work of specialized agencies in the sphere of transport and communications;
(c) To report to the Council, on its request, on the work of any of the 'specialized ·agencies in the sphere of transport and communication;
(d) Tei advises the Council in fields where no permanent international organization yet exists and on problems which concern more than one sphere of transport or communication;
(a) The Committee on Transport and Communications shall comprise one representative of each of the fifteen Members of the United Nations to be appointed by the Council.

(b) In order to ensure a well-balanced representation of the various fields covered by the Commission, the Secretary-General shall consult the Government thus designed, before the appointment of representatives is made by the Government and confirmed by the Council.

(c) A. With the exception of the initial period, the term of office shall be three years. During the initial period, one third of the members shall be appointed for two years, one third for three years and one third for four years, the duration of the term of office of each member being determined by lot.
(d) The outgoing members may be reappointed.

(e) In the event that a member of the Commission is unable to perform his or her duties for the duration of a three-year term, the seat which has become vacant shall be taken by a representative designated by the Government.
2. The functions of the Commission are as follows:
(a) To attend the 1st Economic and Social Council in its work on transport and communications;
(b) To advise the Council on the coordination of the work of the specialized institutions in the field of transport and communications;
(c) To report to the Council, at its request, on the work of all specialized transport and communications institutions;
(d) To advise the Council on areas where there is not yet a permanent internal organization, or on proposals relating to more than one communication channel;
(e) To suggest to the Council the creation of new agencies, or the conclusion of new . conventions or the revision of existing con› ventions;

(f) On instructions of the Economic and Social Council and when so authorized by convention or agreement between. the parties, to perform the task of conciliation in cases of disputes between States and (or) specialized agencies, on problems concerning international transport and · communications where not deal with by other means;
(g) To pe,rform such other Tasks as the Eco› nomic and Social Council may require of it on any question concerning international transport and communicatorio:μs;
(h) To assist the Security Council, if so intended by the Economic and Social Council, · in accordance with Article 65 of the Charter;
(i) To assist the Trusteeship Council, if so desired by the Economic an~ Social Council, in accordance with Article 91 of the Charter.

3. The Temporary Transport and Communications Commission shall continue to function. until the date· fixed by the Council for its re› placement by the permanent Commission, and the functions of the Temporary Conurission will be these listed in paragraph 2 above.

4. The Committee on Negotiations with Inter› governmental Agencies is directed to enter into negotiations as soon as possible through the Provi› Zionist International Civil Aviation Organization, for the purpose of branching it into relationship with the United Nations, and to submit a report of the negotiations to the third session of the Council, including therein a draft · preliminary agreement based upon upon these negotiations.

5. The Economic and Social Council considers that it would be desirable to study further the question of establishing relations between the Economic and Social . Council and inter-governmental› mental agencies in the field of inland transport, in view of the seriousness and complexity of this problem. The Transport and Communications Commission should submit its recommendations to the Economic arid Social Council on the question›tion of ·the forms of relationship and the organization› izations with which the Council should establish relationship.

6. . The Council express the hope that a world conference in the field of telecommunications will be agreed as soon as possible to review the organization of the International Telecom› ammunitions Union and its radio regulations and to enable the International Telecommunications Union to be bought into relationships with the United Nations.

7. The Secretary-General is directed to con. vene, as soon as possible, a meeting of telecom› ammunitions experts designed by the Government› ments of all the countries Members of the United Nations which may wish to participate in such · a meeting, with a view to drafting proposals to be submitted by the respective adn:rinistrations
(e) Propose to the Council the creation of new institutions or the conclusion of new conventions or the revision of existing conventions;

(t) At the request of the Economic and Social Council, and when authorized to do so by a convention or agreement between the parties, act as a conciliation body, when a difference arises between the States and (OR) the institutions specialising in issues› tiom; transport and international communications, and this difference is not regulated by other means;
(g) To carry out any other work to be carried out by the Economic and Social Council in the field of international transport and communications;
(i) To attend the Trusteeship Council, at the request of the Economic and Social Council, in accordance with Article 91 of the Charter.
(h) To assist the Security Coqseil, at the request of the Economic and Social Council, in accordance with Article 65 of the Charter;

3. The Temporary Committee on Transport and Communications will continue to function up to the date fixed by the Council, to which it will be replaced by the Standing Committee, its powers and those set out in paragraph 2 above.

5. The Economic and Social Council considers that it is preferable to carry out a more comprehensive study of the question of establishing relations between the Economic and Social Council and the intergovernmental institutions in the field of internal transport, since it gives importance and complexity to this problem.

6. The Council expressed the hope that an international conference on telecommunications would be convened as soon as possible to review the organization of the International Telecommunications Union and its regulation in the context of the radiotliff use, and to enable this organization to be put in contact with the Organization of the United Nations.

7., The Secretary General is invited to convene, as far as possible, a conference of experts in telecommunications, designed by all the governments of the Member States of the Organization of the United Nations to be part of it, with a view to drawing up the proposals which various administrations will submit to the Conference.
I to the Telecommunications Conference· with 'respect to trunging the International Telecom› munitions Union into relationship with the United Nations. ,

The Secretary-General is directed to the attention of the preliminary five~Power conference on telecommunications, which it is expected will be kept at Moscow, the questions which~will have to be considered in branching the International Telecommunications,Union into relation with the United Nations.

9. The Secretary-General is dirl':cted to con› vene, as soon as possible, , a meeting of postal experts designed by the Governments of all the countries Members of the United Nations which might wish to participate in suc4 a meeting› ing, with a view to · drafting proposals to be submitted by the respective .administrations to the Congress of the Universal Postal Union foreseen for the spring of 194 7, with respect to trunging the Universal ·Postal Union into relationship with the United Nations.

10. The question of the establishment of a world-wide inter-governmental shipping 'organ-· ization to deal with technical matters shall be fully examined by the permanent Transport and Communications Commission with the assistance of the necessary experts. The Commission is informed to report to the Council on the con-. recommendations re-ached on that matter. In the average› time, the Secretary-General is authorized to see the views of the United Maritime C9nsulta› tive Council at its forthcoming meeting in Amsterdam. · .
11. A, committee of experts will be agreed as soon as possible in order to prepare the ground for the meeting of a world conference on the subject of sports and border formalities.

12. The Transport and Communications Com› mission is requested to examine the situation with respect to the co-ordination of activities in the fields of aviation, shipping,. and telecom› ammunitions . in regard to safety at sea and i:p. the air, w~th a view to making recommendations on the mac\_hinery required for the necessary co-ordination of such activities. ·

13. In agreement with the resolution of the General Assumption Charging the Economic and Social Council to assume and continue provi› Zionly the work hierto done by the Economic, Financial and Transit Department of the League of Nations, and having regard to the recommendation› beggings of the Temporary Transport and Communications Commission in this need, the Economic and Social Council directs the Secretary-General to take the following 'steps in order to put into effect these proposals:
. (a) '!'.hat it is suggested~d to the Eur!'.)pean Central Inland Transport Organization that it may continue, for the time being, the study ·of the co-ordination of inland transport.·

text_en: 6. THE RIGHTS OF HOMM IN INTERNATIONAL TREATIES

text_fr: En  attendant  !'adoption  d'une  declaration internationale  des  droits,  il  convient  d'admettre le principe general que les  traites internation,aux interessant les  droits fondamentaux de l'homme, notamment,  clans  toute  la  mesure  du  possible, les  traites · de paix,  se  conformeront  aux normes fondamentales  relatives a ces  'droits  enoncees clans  la Charte.
text_en: Pending the adoption of an international declaration of rights, it must be recognized that the general principle is that internation treaties, inter alia, involving fundamental human rights, as far as possible, will comply with the fundamental norms relating to these 'rights enshrined in the Charter.

text_en: 8. SUB-COMMISSION ON FREEDOM OF' INFORMATION AND OF THE PRESS

items_fr: (a) The  Commission . on  Human  Rights is empowered  to  establish  a  sub-commission  on freedom  of information and of the press.

items_en: (a) The Commission . on Human Rights is empowered to establish a sub-commission on freedom of information and of the press.

(b) The function of the sub-commission shall be,. in the first instant~, to examine what rights, obligations and practices should be in› closed in the concept of freedom of information, and to report to the Commission on Human Rights on any issues that may· arise from such examination.

text_fr: 8. Sous-CoMMISION  DE  LA  LIBERTE  DE L'INFORMATION ET DE  LA  PRESSE
text_en: 8. SUB-COMMISION OF FREEDOM OF INFORMATION AND THE PRESS

items_fr: a) . La  Cominission  des  droits  de  l'homm~ a le  droit  d'instituer une  Sous-Commission  de  la liberte  de  !'information et de  la presse.

b)  En  premier  lieu,  la  Sous-Commission  a · pour attributions d'examiner quels droits, quelles obligations  et  quelles  coutumes  doivent  relever de  la notion de  liberte  d'information et de faire rapport a la  Commission des  droits  de  l'homme, sur tous  les  problemes qui pourraient se  degager au cours  de  cet  exam~n.
items_en: (a) . The Committee on Human Rights has the right to establish a Sub-Commission on Freedom of Information and the Press.

(b) First, the Sub-Commission shall have the task of examining which rights, obligations and customs should fall within the concept of freedom of information and of reporting to the Commission on Human Rights on any problems which may arise during this review.

items_fr: a)  La  Commission  des  droits  de  l'homme a le  droit  d'instituer une  Sous-Commission  de  la protection des  rninorites.

( b) Unless the Cominission otherwise decides, . the  function  of  the  sub-commission  shall  be, in the first  instance,  to examine what provisions should  be  adopted  in  the  definition  of  the principles  which  are  to· be  applied  in the  field of  protection  of  minorities,  and  to  deal  with the  urgent  problems  in  this  field  by  making recommendations to· the Commission.

b) A  moins  que  la  Commission  n'en decide autrer:nent,  la  Sous-Commission  aura pour attri› butions,  en premier lieu,  d'examiner ,les  disposi› tions a prendre en  ce  qui conceme la definition des  principes a appliquer en rnatiere de  protec› tion  des  minorites,  de  s'occuper des  problemes urgents qui se posent dans ce domaine et d'adres.  ser  des  recommandations a la  Commission a ce sujet.
items_en: (a) The Commission on Human Rights has the right to establish a Sub-Commission on the Protection of Rninorites.

(b) Unless the Committee otherwise decisions, . the function of the sub-committee shall be, in the first instance, to examine what provisions should be adopted in the definition of the principals which are to be applied in the field of protection of minorities, and to deal with the urgent problems in this field by making recommendations to the Commission.

(b) Unless the Commission decides otherwise, the first aim of the Sub-Commission shall be to examine the provisions to be adopted in relation to the definition of the principles to be applied in the field of protection of minorities, to deal with urgent problems in this field and to make recommendations to the Commission on this subject.

10. Sub-COMMISSION ON THE ABOLITION OF DISTINCTIONS

items_fr: a)  La Commission  des  droits  de  l'homme est habilitee' a creer  une  Sous-Commission  pour !'abolition des  distinctions  fondees  sur  la  race, le  sexe,  la langue  ou  la  religion.

b) A  moins  que la Commission  n'en decide autrement,  la  Sous-Cominission  aura  d'abord pour  attributions  d'examiner Jes -dispositions a prendre  en  ce  qui  conceme  la  definition  des principes a appliquer  dans  la  lutte  contre  les distinctions,  de s'occuper des  problemes  urgents qui se  posent dans ce  domaine,  et d'adresser des recomrµandations a la  Commission a ce  sujet.
items_en: (a) The Commission on Human Rights is empowered to establish a Sub-Commission to abolish distinctions based on race, sex, language or religion.

(b) Unless the Commission decides otherwise, the first task of the Sub-Committee shall be to examine the provisions to be taken with regard to the definition of the principles to be applied in the fight against distinctions, to deal with urgent problems in this field, and to make recommendations to the Commission on this subject.

items_en: (a) The Social Commission shall consist of one representative from each of eighteen
It will force the international social problems to be solved, without losing sight of the relationship between these problems and the development of social activities within each country.

(b) The activities of the United Nations in the social sphere must be based on democratic principles; they must exercise the interests of all the social groups concerned and with the active participation of the organizations which ensure the union of each group (workers' unions, agricultural associations, etc.).

(c) The raising of the standard of living and the well-being of the peoples of the States Members of the United Nations, which must include not only wages and income, but also all kinds of social services, are an important task for the United Nations.

(d) Special attention must be paid to the social problems of the countries which need to be addressed, and in particular those whose territories have been occupied by the nemi, in order to establish as soon as possible the normal conditions.
(e) (i) To be successful, the activities of the United Nations in the social field require the constant collaboration of specialized institutions.
(ii) One of the functions of the United Nations is to coordinate the activities of specialized agencies with a view to avoiding gaps and duplication.
(iii) The United Nations should consider the possibility of assuming responsibility for social activities which do not fall within the competence of any existing specialized institution.

3. QUALIFICATIONS OF THE RESOURCE OF THE COMMISSION OF SOCIAL QUALIFICATIONS

(a) The observations made by the Temporary Commission on Social Questions concerning the provisions to be taken in the field of social security and contained in Section 11 of its report, and its suggestions on methods of carrying out these tasks, shall be referred to the Social Affairs Committee, which shall examine them as soon as possible and shall make recommendations to the Economic and Social Council.

(b) The observations and recommendations concerning the work of the Society of Nations in the social field, contained in section 14 of the report of the Temporary Committee on Social Affairs, shall be referred to the Social Affairs Committee, taking into account the conditions prevailing in the post-war world: '

(i) To study the best way to follow up the activities of the Society of Nations with regard to trafficking in women and children, as well as all measures taken to punish trafficking in women and children;

(ii) To study how the work on child protection can be carried out effectively, in collaboration with international organizations dealing with the specific aspects of child protection issues, and to take steps towards the establishment of a specially constituted sub-committee to deal with child protection;
(ii) To examine what effective mechanism can be adopted with a view to examining, from a broad international point of view, the means of preventing crime and the treatment of offenders;

text_en: Decides to confer on the Sub-Commission the status of a commission; this commission shall be established on the Commission on the Status of Women.

items_fr: . La Commission a  pour fonctlons  de  presenter · des  recommandations  et  rapports  au  Conseil. economique  et social  sur le  developpement  des droits  de la femme  dans les  domaines  politigue, economique, social et de !'instruction.  La Com› mission  formulera  egalement  des  recommanda› tions  sur  les  problemes  p'resentant un  caractere d'urgence  dans  le  domaine  des  droits.  de  la femme.
items_en: The Commission's task is to present recommendations and reports to the Council on the development of women's rights in the political, economic, social and educational fields. The Commission will also make recommendations on problems of urgency in the field of women's rights.

text_fr: The Commission m,ay  submit proposals to  the Council  regarding its  terms  of  reference.
text_en: The Commission m,ay submitted proposals to the Council looking at its terms of reference.

items_fr: (a) The Commission on the Status of Women shall  consist  of  one  representative  from  each  of fifteen  Members  of  the  United Nations selected by the Council.

( b) With a  view to securing a  balanced rep› resentation  in  the  various  fields  covered  by  the Commission,  the Secretary-General shall consult with  the  Governments  so  selected  before  the representatives  are  finally  nominated  by  these Governments and confirmed by the CounciL

( c) Except' for  the  initial  period,  the  term of office  shall be for  three years.  For the initial period,  one-third of  the members shall serve for two  years,  one-third  for  three  years,  and  one› third  for  four  years,  the  term  of  each  member to  be  determined  by  lot.
( d) Retiring  members  shall  be  eligible  for re-electicm.

( e) In the event that a  member of the Com› mission  is  unable to  serve  for  the full  three-year term,  the vacancy thus arising shall be filled  by a representative  designated  by  the  Member Government, subject to the provisions of paragraph  ( b) above.

text_fr: In order  to  assist  the  Commission  on  the Status  of  Women,  the  Secretary-General  is  re› quested  to  make  arrangements  for  a  complete and detailed  study of  the legislation  concerning the status  of  women  and  the  practical  applica› tion of such legislation.
text_en: In order to assist the Commission on the Status of Women, the Secretary-General is re› quested to make arrangements for a complete and detailed study of the legislation concerning the status of women and the practical application of such legislation.

3. PoLITIQUE A SUIVRE  ET  PROGRAMME
items_en: (a) The Commission on the Status of Women shall comprise one representative of each of the fifteen Members of the United Nations to be designated by the Council.

(b) In order to ensure a well-balanced representation of the various fields dealt with by the Commission, the Secretary-General shall consult the Government thus designed, before the appointment of representatives is made by the Governments and entrusted by the Council.

(c) With the exception of the initial period, the duration of the term of office shall be three years. During the initial period, one third of the members shall be appointed for two years, one third for three years and one third for four years, the duration of the term of office of each member being determined by lot.
(d) Outgoing members may be appointed anew.

(e) In the event that a member of the Comrnissi is unable to perform his or her duties for the duration of a three-year term, the seat which has become vacant shall be occupied by a representative designated by the Government of the Member State, in accordance with the provisions of paragraph (b) above. ·
3. POLITICAL FOLLOW-UP AND PROGRAMME

text_en: Sections I and II of the report of the Sub› Committee, relating to the policy to be followed and the programme of work, are referred to the Commission on the Status of Women for consideration.

text_fr: En vue  d'assister la  Commission  de la condi› tion  de  la  ,femme,  le  Secretaire  general  est invite a prendre  toutes  dispositions  permettant de faire  une etude complete et detaillee  des  lois concemant la  condition  de  la femme  ainsi  que de  leur  application.
text_en: With a view to assisting the Commission on the Status of Women, the Secretary-General is invited to take all necessary measures to ensure that laws concerning the status of women and their application are fully studied and detailed.

b) Afin  d'assurer  une  representation  bien equilibree des  differents domaines  dont s'occupe la  Conunission,  le  Secretaire  general  consultera les  Gouvernements ainsi  design6s,  avant que la nomination  des  representants  ne  soit  f~ite  de fac;on  definitive par ces  Gouvernements et confir› mee par le Conseil.

c) A  }'exception  de  la  periode  initiale,  la duree du mandat sera de  trois  annees.  Pendant 11!periode  initiale,  un  tiers  des  membres  seront nommes  pour deux  ans,  un tiers  pour trois  ans et  un tiers  pour quatre ans,  la duree du mandat de  chaque  membre  etant determinee  par tirage au sort.
d) Les  membres  sortants pourront @tre  nom› mes a nouveau.

items_en: (a) The Social Commission shall consist of one representative from each of eighteen Members of the United Nations selected by the Council. ·
( b) With a view to securing a balanced representation› feeling in the various fields covered by the Commission, the Secretary-General will consult with the Governments so selected before the

(b) In order to ensure a well-balanced representation of the different areas dealt with by the Commission, the Secretary-General shall consult with the Governments, where appropriate, before the appointment of representatives is made final by those Governments and shall be entrusted by the Council.
(c) With the exception of the initial period, the term of office shall be three years.
(d) Outgoing members may be appointed anew.

(e) In the case of a member of the Commission who would not be in a position to perform his or her functions for the duration of a term of three years, the seat which has become vacant shall be occupied by a representative designated by the Government of the Member State, in accordance with the provisions of paragraph (b) above.

(f) The Economic and Social Council may also establish, in its individual capacity, not more than 12 corresponding members from countries which are not represented in the Commission, who shall be appointed with the approval of the Governments concerned.
4 .. COMMISSION ON HUMAN RIGHTS
(a) The Commission on Human Rights shall comprise one representative of each of the eighteen members of the United Nations to be designated by the Council.

(b) In order to ensure a well-balanced representation of the different areas covered by the Conunission, the Secretary-General shall consult with the Governments so designed before the appointment of representatives is made in a manner which is final by those Governments and entrusted by the Council.

(c) With the exception of the initial period, the term of office shall be three years. For the initial period, one third of the members shall be appointed for two years, one third for three years and one third for four years, the duration of the term of office of each member being determined by lot.
(d) Outgoing members will be able to be named again.

(e) In the event that a member of the Commission is unable to serve for the duration of a three-year term, the seat which has become vacant shall be occupied by the Government of the Member State, in accordance with the provisions of paragraph (b) above.

text_fr: Requests the  Secretary-General  to  offer  all possible  assistance to the Director-General of the Food and Agriculture Organization at all stages, in making the survey and in preparing proposals concerning  longer-term  international  machinery with  reference  to  food,  with  a  view  to  ensuring that  these  proposals  are  in  harmony  with  the broad  pattern  of  the  United  Nations  for  inter› national  economic  organization  and  co-opera› tion;  and  requests  the  Secretary-General  to report to  the  next session  of  the  Council.

text_en: Taking note that the report of the special conference and, in particular, the "Recommendations on the organization has provided for a less immediate future", invites the Director-General of the Food and Agriculture Organisation to carry out an inquiry and to submit proposals for a longer-term international policy, and requests him to be held in this capacity and in close liaison with the Economic and Social Council;

text_fr: Demande au  Secretaire  general  de  fournir constamment toute !'assistance possible au Direc› teur general  de  !'Organisation de l'alimentation et de !'agriculture, dans la conduite des  enquetes et  l' elaboration  des  propositions  sur la politique internationale a long terme touchant l'alimenta› tion,  ery.  vue  de  mettre  ces  propositions  en  har› monie avec la ligne de conduite generate adoptee par !'Organisation des  Nations  Unies  en  ce  qui conceme }'organisation et la cooperation econo› miques internationales; et demande au Secretaire general de presenter  un rapport a ce  sujet a la prochaine  session  du  Conseil.
text_en: Requests the Secretary-General to provide all possible assistance to the Director-General of the Food and Agriculture Organization, in the conduct of investigations and the elaboration of proposals on long-term international food policy, with a view to putting these proposals in line with the general policy adopted by the United Nations in relation to international economic cooperation and organization; and requests the Secretary-General to submit a report on this matter to the next session of the Council.
